$comment: Shared type definitions referenced by all front matter schemas via $ref. These formalize the enums, objects, and patterns documented in STYLE_GUIDE.md (Front Matter Conventions).

id.$comment: Lowercase identifier with hyphens, used for stage IDs, artifact names, and feeds_into references.

file-path.$comment: Relative path to a markdown file within the repository (e.g., stages/initiation/checklist.md).

stage-execution-pattern.$comment: Foundational (once per project), iterative (per increment), or terminal (once at project end — Closure).

checkpoint-protocol.$comment: The six checkpoint protocols defining how each checkpoint is processed. Describes the execution procedure, not the checkpoint type (Gate/Review/Alignment).

role.$comment: Role identifiers from the RACI matrix in guides/roles.md. Ordered by first accountability appearance in the SDLC pipeline.

checkpoint-type.$comment: Checkpoint classification: gate (investment decision, hard stop), review (quality/readiness check), alignment (non-blocking synchronization).

checkpoint-condition.$comment: Named project-level condition gating whether a checkpoint applies. Omitted when the checkpoint always applies. compliance: the project has regulatory, legal, or security sign-off requirements (typically Standard or Enterprise risk tier).

checkpoint.$comment: A stage checkpoint with type, protocol, display name, responsible roles, and optional accountable role (RACI 'A' — the decision-maker). accountable_role is used on gate-type checkpoints where the decision authority differs from the preparers. condition names a project-level condition gating whether the checkpoint applies; omit it when the checkpoint always applies.

output.$comment: A stage output artifact. template points to a fillable template; embedded_in names the parent artifact when this output is a section rather than a standalone file.

autonomy-level.$comment: AI autonomy tier from guides/ai-assistance.md. Determines how much independent action the agent takes.

oversight-intensity.$comment: How closely a human monitors agent work: active (review each step), passive (review outputs), minimal (spot-check only).

working-location-type.$comment: Where the stage's primary work happens: artifacts (docs/ governance files) or source-code (application codebase).

raci-roles.$comment: RACI matrix assignments for a stage. Maps Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed to role arrays. Mirrors guides/framework.md.
